summary: Iranian forces launched 12 ballistic missiles at the US al‑Azraq base in Jordan and closed the Strait of Hormuz, while Israel reported a hostile aircraft intrusion near Hebron and conducted an exercise in the southern Hebron area. An Israeli strike hit the outskirts of Taraiyya in Lebanon's eastern Beqaa Valley and a suspected Hezbollah drone was intercepted over southern Lebanon, triggering sirens in Shlomi. Protests erupted in Jerusalem and the West Bank, and Gulf states reported air‑defence activity and a fire on an oil tanker in Oman.
key_points: Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps fired 12 ballistic missiles at the US‑operated al‑Azraq air base in Jordan (Source: @presstv/193626).
The IDF announced alerts for hostile aircraft intrusion in the southern Hebron area, with details under investigation (Source: @idf_telegram/24286).
Israel struck the outskirts of Taraiyya in Lebanon's eastern Beqaa Valley, approximately 90 km from the Israeli border (Source: @manniefabian/58542).
A suspected Hezbollah drone was intercepted over southern Lebanon, triggering sirens in the Israeli border community of Shlomi (Source: @manniefabian/58536).
Iran’s Waterway Administration announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz until further notice (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/368923).
The US Navy stated that no US warship was hit, contradicting Iranian claims (Source: @asafroz/50162).
Kuwait’s General Staff reported air‑defence systems actively intercepting hostile aerial targets (Source: @shin_persian/6919).

sections.executive_summary: Iranian forces launched 12 ballistic missiles at the US al‑Azraq base in Jordan and closed the Strait of Hormuz, while Israel reported a hostile aircraft intrusion near Hebron and conducted a drill in the southern Hebron area. An Israeli strike hit the outskirts of Taraiyya in Lebanon's eastern Beqaa Valley and a suspected Hezbollah drone was intercepted over southern Lebanon, triggering sirens in Shlomi. Protests erupted in Jerusalem and the West Bank, and Gulf states reported air‑defence activity and a fire on an oil tanker in Oman.

points: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced it launched 12 ballistic missiles targeting the US‑operated al‑Azraq air base in Jordan as a response to US missile attacks (Source: @presstv/193626).
Iran’s Waterway Administration declared the Strait of Hormuz closed indefinitely, halting all maritime traffic through the strategic waterway (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/368923).
Three individuals were reported injured in incidents linked to the US‑Iran conflict, according to the head of Tehran’s emergency organization (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/368889).
Additional injuries were mentioned in a later report, though details were not disclosed (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/368925).
The US Navy refuted Iranian claims, stating that no US warship was damaged in recent hostilities (Source: @asafroz/50162).
The IDF spokesperson said alerts were activated for a hostile aircraft intrusion in the southern Hebron region, with investigations ongoing (Source: @idf_telegram/24286).

A large‑scale military exercise began in the southern Hebron area under the Yehuda Brigade, involving significant troop movement, vehicle traffic, and live‑fire sounds, scheduled to end by midday (Source: @idf_telegram/24289).
Israeli forces carried out an airstrike on the outskirts of the town of Taraiyya in Lebanon’s eastern Beqaa Valley, roughly 90 km from the Israeli border (Source: @manniefabian/58542).
A suspected Hezbollah drone was intercepted over southern Lebanon, prompting sirens in the Israeli border community of Shlomi (Source: @manniefabian/58536).
Press reports indicated Israeli drones targeted a civilian area between the villages of Hazin and Tarya in Lebanon’s eastern Bekaa region (Source: @presstv/193630).

The Jerusalem faction announced a series of protests and road blockades set to begin at 17:00, triggered by the arrest and transfer of yeshiva students to a military prison, with expected significant traffic disruptions across central Gush Dan (Source: @yediotnews25/131156).
Protesters in the West Bank were urged to avoid gatherings and movement in residential areas, following security force instructions amid heightened tension (Source: @almogboker78/17447).

Five individuals were reported injured in early‑morning attacks across Iran, with three injuries in Tehran and two in Hormozgan; all were released after medical treatment (Source: @SharghDaily/165242).
A video from VahidOnline showed explosions near the Karaj airport at 03:56, suggesting possible aerial or missile activity in the vicinity (Source: @kianmeli1/87913).
Lebanese media reported an Israeli strike in the eastern Beqaa Valley near the town of Taraiyya, approximately 90 km from the Israeli border (Source: @manniefabian/58542).
A suspected Hezbollah drone was intercepted over southern Lebanon, triggering civil defense sirens in the Israeli border town of Shlomi (Source: @manniefabian/58536).
Pro‑Hezbollah demonstrations took place in Beirut, showing support for the Iranian regime (Source: @lelotsenzura/92838).
Israeli drones were reported to have targeted a civilian area between the villages of Hazin and Tarya in Lebanon’s eastern Bekaa region (Source: @presstv/193630).

Kuwait’s General Staff announced that its air‑defence systems are actively intercepting hostile aerial targets and urged the public to follow official safety instructions (Source: @shin_persian/6919).
An oil tanker reported a fire on board while docked at the port of Shinas in Oman, as shown in footage released by PressTV (Source: @presstv/193638).
Iran’s Waterway Administration declared the Strait of Hormuz closed, affecting Gulf maritime traffic (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/368923).
Reports indicated that US‑operated commercial vessels continue to transit the Strait of Hormuz despite Iranian statements (Source: @asafroz/50161).

key_developments: Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps launched 12 ballistic missiles at the US‑operated al‑Azraq air base in Jordan (Source: @presstv/193626).
The IDF issued alerts for a hostile aircraft intrusion in the southern Hebron area, with investigations ongoing (Source: @idf_telegram/24286).
Israel conducted an airstrike on the outskirts of Taraiyya in Lebanon’s eastern Beqaa Valley, about 90 km from the Israeli border (Source: @manniefabian/58542).
A suspected Hezbollah drone was intercepted over southern Lebanon, triggering sirens in Shlomi (Source: @manniefabian/58536).
Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz indefinitely, halting maritime traffic (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/368923).
The US Navy stated no US warship was hit, contradicting Iranian claims (Source: @asafroz/50162).
Kuwait’s General Staff reported active air‑defence interceptions of hostile aerial targets (Source: @shin_persian/6919).
sections.threat_assessment: Iran’s missile launch against a US base in Jordan and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz indicate a heightened willingness to project power and disrupt regional logistics. Israel’s cross‑border strikes and interception of Hezbollah drones demonstrate an escalating air‑ground contest on the Lebanon frontier. Gulf states are reinforcing air‑defence postures, while civilian protests in the West Bank and Jerusalem add internal instability, creating a multi‑dimensional threat environment.
sections.regional_response: The United States has publicly denied any damage to its naval assets and continues to operate commercial shipping through the Hormuz corridor, while diplomatic channels remain active. Kuwait and Oman have issued safety advisories and reported defensive actions, reflecting regional vigilance. Israeli military exercises and strikes aim to deter Hezbollah activity, prompting Lebanese authorities to monitor border incidents closely.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring should prioritize satellite imagery of the Strait of Hormuz for any re‑opening signals and track missile trajectories from Iran. Continued surveillance of southern Lebanon is essential to detect further drone activity and potential Hezbollah retaliation. Israeli drill movements near Hebron warrant close observation for escalation patterns. Gulf air‑defence alerts suggest an increased risk of aerial incursions, requiring real‑time data sharing among allied forces.
